title: Quorl Matrin

{{Quote|That man over there! It's Quorl Matrin. He's an Imperial governor. We served in the Senate together.|Leia Organa|<ref name="marvel70" />}}
'''Quorl Matrin''' was the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Stenos/Legends|Stenos]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Formerly an [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senator]], Matrin led the Imperial invasion and occupation of Stenos, which succeeded due partly to the complete apathy of the [[Stenax]] natives. Afterwards, Matrin—a lover of archaeology and avid collector of obscure artifacts—became obsessed with finding an ancient lost statue of the Stenax deity [[Vol]], eventually hiring [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Rik Duel/Legends|Rik Duel]] to find the idol. Duel's discovery of the statue finally roused the ire of the Stenaxes, who ripped Matrin limb from limb in order to regain their artifact.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I'm surprised you weren't murdered years ago!|Quorl Matrin, to Rik Duel|<ref name="marvel70" />}}
[[File:Vol_idol.jpg|thumb|left|175px|The statue of Vol that became Quorl Matrin's obsession.]]
Matrin previously served in the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]], where he became acquainted with [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan's]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. A close friend of [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Bertroff Hissa]], Matrin was eventually named the [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Stenos/Legends|Stenos]], a world with a history that piqued his interest—an avid collector of esoteric artifacts, Matrin had long been intrigued by the legend of the native [[Stenax]]es' lost statue of their god [[Vol]]. Matrin and his troops had little trouble securing the world despite the Stenaxes' imposing size and martial prowess, as the natives befuddled Matrin by seemingly not paying any attention at all to the Imperial occupation.<ref name="gamer1" /> The successful invasion also chased out an active [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] cell led by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Pejanes Kindar]].<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Matrin hired a group of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] led by [[Rik Duel/Legends|Rik Duel]] in order to track down the statue of Vol, which he had grown to covet.<ref name="marvel70" />

Duel and his gang eventually achieved this through the unwitting aid of Rebels [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], who had arrived on Stenos to investigate Kindar's disappearance. After finding the statue—buried in a temple that formerly served as Kindar's base—Matrin moved his [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] in to attack the Rebels but kept watch on Duel, having never trusted the man. That allowed him to discover Duel attempting to escape Stenos with the statue, cutting him off with a company of soldiers at his back. Duel handed the artifact over, but as Matrin prepared to execute the smuggler, he was interrupted by the pounding wings of hundreds of Stenaxes determined to regain their important religious totem.<ref name="marvel70" /> As the man holding the statue at the time, Matrin became the direct target of the Stenax attack. Although he was presumed [[Death/Legends|killed]], his remains were too badly disfigured to allow for proper identification.<ref name="gamer1" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Governor Matrin has been so anxious to add to his private collection of esoteric artifacts.|Rik Duel|<ref name="marvel70" />}}
Possessing an appropriately domineering and superior attitude for an Imperial governor, Quorl Matrin was cunning and duplicitous, attributes that served him well when dealing with lowlifes like Rik Duel. Matrin was a collector of obscure artifacts and went to great lengths to acquire them—<ref name="marvel70" /> the Stenax statue of Vol was a particular obsession, a piece Matrin sought for years. Matrin also became deeply interested in Stenax culture itself, particularly the natives' refusal to acknowledge the Imperial occupiers, to the point where it became a major goal of Matrin's to get the Stenax to react to him in any way. Matrin had a warm and friendly relationship with Grand Moff Bertroff Hissa, calling him by the nickname "Troff."<ref name="gamer1" /> Matrin and Hissa shared a passion for archaeology,<ref name="alien">{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-1|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> and Matrin often amused Hissa with impressions of his superiors, including one of High Prophet [[Jedgar]] that Hissa found particularly hilarious.<ref name="gamer1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Quorl Matrin appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 70|''Star Wars'' (1977) 70]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]], illustrated by [[Kerry Gammill]] and [[Tom Palmer]] and released in [[1983]].<ref name="marvel70" />
